_source: Core Rules pp.128-130 — Character Events Table (D100)
_note: Roll D100 on a random non-Bot, non-Soulless crew member. If crew has Precursor members, they roll twice and pick either. Species exceptions noted per entry.
name: character_events

result.name: Violence is Depressing
result.description: All this endless violence is depressing you. The character refuses to participate in any battle during the next campaign turn (except for an Invasion battle). Get +1 story point, as they get a new perspective on life.

result.name: Business Elsewhere
result.description: You have some business to attend to elsewhere. The character is unavailable for the next two campaign turns. They require no Upkeep during this time, and cannot be the target of any events. When they return, award them 1D6 XP, and roll once on the Loot Table.

species_exceptions.Swift: They never return but are replaced with a brand new Swift character rolled up normally

result.name: Local Friends
result.description: You make some local friends. The character earns +1 XP.

result.name: Time to Move On
result.description: You are starting to wonder if it is time to move on. If the character is currently in Sick Bay, roll 1D6. If the roll is equal or below the number of campaign turns of recovery left, they will decide to leave the crew.

result.name: Letter from Home
result.description: You get a letter from home. The character earns +1 XP. Roll 1D6. On a 5-6 you immediately receive a Quest.

result.name: Argue with Crew
result.description: You argue with the rest of the crew. During the next campaign turn, the crew member refuses to do any tasks but will participate in battle normally.

result.name: Scrap with Crewmate
result.description: You get in a scrap with another crew member. Randomly select another crew member and roll 1D6+Combat Skill for each. The lower score must spend one campaign turn in Sick Bay. On a draw, both go to Sick Bay.

species_exceptions.K'Erin: If a K'Erin is in the crew, you must fight them

result.name: Good Food
result.description: The local food is sitting well with you. If in Sick Bay, reduce your recovery time by one campaign turn. If not, earn +1 XP.

result.name: Not the Same Person
result.description: You are not sure you are really the same person any longer. Roll on the Motivation Table (p.26) and change to that motivation. You do not receive any items or resources, but if the new motivation would have any ability score bonuses, earn +1 XP for each +1 ability score you would have received normally. If you roll the current motivation, the character becomes particularly self-assured. Earn +1 story point.

result.name: Make-over
result.description: The character changes their clothes, hair-style, or something else. This is only cosmetic. If you are able to, reflect this on the miniature.

result.name: Overhear Something Useful
result.description: Overhear something useful. Earn 1 Quest Rumor.

result.name: Earn on the Side
result.description: Earn a little on the side. Receive +2 credits.

result.name: Heart to Heart
result.description: Have a heart to heart talk with a crew member. Select a random crew member. Both earn +1 XP.

result.name: Exercise
result.description: Time spent exercising is never wasted. Earn +2 XP.

result.name: Unusual Hobby
result.description: You pick up an unusual hobby. Earn +1 story point and the character talks about it constantly.

result.name: Scars Tell the Story
result.description: The scars tell the story. If the character was injured in any way last or this campaign turn, they earn +2 XP.

result.name: Time to Reflect
result.description: You've had time to reflect on your adventures. Earn +1D3 XP.

result.name: Personal Breakthrough
result.description: You've made a real personal breakthrough. Select one ability score that has not been increased from its starting value when the character entered the campaign. It increases by +1 immediately.

result.name: Hurt Working on Ship
result.description: You get hurt while working on the ship. Spend one campaign turn in Sick Bay, and the ship takes 1 Hull Point damage.

result.name: Found True Love
result.description: You have finally found true love in this bleak universe. If the character's motivation was True Love, they earn +1D6 XP. Regardless, get +1 story point. And it's a rather nice thought, isn't it?

result.name: Personal Enemy
result.description: A personal enemy has tracked you down. Add a Rival. If this character leaves your crew, the Rival also leaves.

result.name: Gift
result.description: Someone has sent you a gift. Roll once on the Loot Table (p.131).

result.name: Feel Great
result.description: You feel great. Eating well must be paying off. Put a note on your record sheet: Next time this character would be forced to roll on the Injury Table, the roll is ignored.

result.name: Someone Who Knows Someone
result.description: You know someone who knows someone. Add a Patron.

result.name: Charmed Existence
result.description: Maybe you are leading a charmed existence? Add +1 Luck.

result.name: Hard Work
result.description: You've put in a lot of hard work around here. Either repair 2 Hull Point damage or Repair one damaged item in the inventory.

result.name: Don't Make Them Like They Used To
result.description: They don't make them like they used to. A random item carried by the character is damaged, and must be Repaired before it can be used again.

result.name: Where Did It Go
result.description: A random item carried by the character has been lost. When rolling for a Character Event next campaign turn, roll 1D6+Savvy as well. On a 5+, the item turns up again, otherwise it is lost for good.

result.name: Melancholy
result.description: A deep feeling of melancholy and despair is afflicting you. The character will earn no XP next campaign turn.

result.name: Time to Burn
result.description: You've had a lot of time to burn. Next campaign turn, the character may perform an additional action, even if they are in Sick Bay.
